title: Refractory Kawasaki Disease
chief_complaint: Fever for 7 days (39-40°C). Developed red eyes, red cracked lips, strawberry tongue, rash on trunk, swollen hands and feet, and swollen lymph node on one side of neck. Given IVIG 2g/kg on day 6 but still febrile 36 hours later.

physical_exam.Oral: Erythematous cracked lips. Strawberry tongue. Diffuse oropharyngeal erythema.
physical_exam.Extremities: Erythema and edema of hands and feet (acute phase). Periungual desquamation beginning.
physical_exam.Skin: Polymorphous rash on trunk (maculopapular). No vesicles.
physical_exam.Cardiac: Tachycardia. No murmur currently. No gallop.
physical_exam.Labs: CRP 180, ESR 95, WBC 18,500, Albumin 2.5, Platelets 650,000. Echo: Normal coronaries (Z-score <2) on day 5. Repeat echo day 10: RCA Z-score 3.5 (dilated).

correct_diagnosis: Kawasaki Disease - Refractory (IVIG-Resistant) with Early Coronary Artery Dilation
key_learning_points: Kawasaki = acute medium-vessel vasculitis of unknown etiology. Leading cause of acquired heart disease in children in developed countries

Diagnosis: Fever ≥5 days + ≥4 of 5 criteria (Conjunctivitis, Oral changes, Rash, Extremity changes, Cervical lymphadenopathy). Incomplete Kawasaki: Fever + <4 criteria + echo or lab findings suggestive
IVIG 2g/kg single infusion within 10 days reduces coronary aneurysm risk from 25% to 5%. PLUS Aspirin 80-100mg/kg/day divided QID (high dose) until afebrile, then 3-5mg/kg/day (antiplatelet)

Refractory (IVIG-resistant): Persistent or recrudescent fever ≥36h after IVIG. 10-20% of patients. Second dose IVIG 2g/kg. If still refractory: Corticosteroids (Methylprednisolone 30mg/kg IV ×1-3 days) or Infliximab (TNF inhibitor)

Coronary risk stratification: Z-score <2 (no involvement), 2-2.5 (dilation only), 2.5-5 (small aneurysm), 5-10 (medium aneurysm), >10 (giant aneurysm). Aspirin alone for Z<2.5. Add Clopidogrel or Warfarin for medium-giant aneurysms. Echo at diagnosis, 2 weeks, 6-8 weeks, 6 months, 1 year

hidden_data.cbc: WBC: 12,500 /µL | RBC: 4.5 M/µL | Hb: 13.8 g/dL | Hct: 41% | MCV: 88 fL | Platelets: 250,000 /µL
